# 🚀 Pull Request

## 📌 Overview
Provide a clear and concise description of what this PR does.

Explain:
- What was changed
- Why it was changed
- Any important context

---

## 🔗 Related Issue
Closes #(issue number)

If no issue exists, explain why.

---

## 🎯 Type of Change
Select all that apply:

- [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality)
- [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that causes existing functionality to break)
- [ ] 🎨 UI/UX improvement (styling or frontend changes)
- [ ] ⚡ Performance improvement
- [ ] ♻️ Refactor (no functional change)
- [ ] 📚 Documentation update
- [ ] 🔧 Chore / maintenance

---

## 🧠 Motivation & Context
Why is this change needed?

- What problem does it solve?
- What user/business value does it bring?

---

## 🛠️ Implementation Details
Explain how you implemented this change.

Include:
- Key logic changes
- Architecture decisions
- Any tradeoffs made

---

## 🧪 Testing
Describe how you tested your changes:

- [ ] Unit tests added/updated
- [ ] Manually tested in browser
- [ ] Tested on mobile
- [ ] Tested edge cases

### Test Evidence (if applicable)
Include screenshots, logs, or recordings.

---

## 📸 Screenshots / Demos
### Before:
(If applicable)

### After:
(If applicable)

---

## ⚠️ Breaking Changes
- [ ] No breaking changes
- [ ] Yes (describe below)

If yes:
- What breaks?
- Migration steps?

---

## 🔒 Security Considerations
- Any security implications?
- Input validation changes?
- Auth / permissions affected?

---

## 📋 Checklist (Required before merging)

- [ ] Code follows project structure and conventions
- [ ] Self-reviewed my own code
- [ ] No console errors or warnings
- [ ] Works on desktop and mobile
- [ ] No hardcoded secrets or sensitive data
- [ ] Documentation updated (if needed)
- [ ] Linted and formatted properly

---

## 🧩 Additional Notes
Anything else reviewers should know.
